// @generated — labelle-assembler null backend (headless game runner)
// No window, no GPU, no input, no audio, no renderer interaction. The
// main loop runs for a fixed number of frames (LABELLE_NULL_FRAMES env
// var, default 5) at a constant 1/60s timestep, then exits cleanly so
// `defer`-bound teardown (PluginControllers.deinit, GPA leak check,
// preview `bye`) actually runs.
//
// Why a frame counter instead of `window.shouldQuit()`: there is
// no window. Why no `g.render()`: no draw surface. Why no sleep: tests
// want determinism; CI wants a bounded wall-clock. The preview control
// plane (LABELLE_PREVIEW=host:port → hello/heartbeat/bye) still wires
// up because the engine's editor protocol is GPU-independent.
const screen_w: u32 = {{width}};
const screen_h: u32 = {{height}};
const screen_title = "{{title}}";
const target_fps: u32 = {{fps}};

const DEFAULT_NULL_FRAMES: u32 = 5;

{{module_vars}}
/// Read LABELLE_NULL_FRAMES from the environment, falling back to
/// DEFAULT_NULL_FRAMES when unset, unparseable, or on any allocation
/// failure. Bounded so a typo (`LABELLE_NULL_FRAMES=infinity`) never
/// deadlocks CI.
///
/// std.process.getEnvVarOwned was removed in Zig 0.16; env flows
/// through std.process.Environ which would require Init plumbing.
/// Fall back to libc getenv — engine links libc, so std.c is available.
fn getMaxFrames(_: std.mem.Allocator) u32 {
    const raw_c = std.c.getenv("LABELLE_NULL_FRAMES") orelse return DEFAULT_NULL_FRAMES;
    const raw = std.mem.span(raw_c);
    return std.fmt.parseInt(u32, raw, 10) catch DEFAULT_NULL_FRAMES;
}

pub fn main() !void {
    var gpa = std.heap.DebugAllocator(.{}).init;
    defer _ = gpa.deinit();
    const allocator = gpa.allocator();

{{hooks_init_block}}
    var g = AssembledGame.init(allocator);
    defer g.deinit();

    g.setHooks(&hooks);

    g.setScreenHeight(@as(f32, @floatFromInt(screen_h)));
    g.getCamera().setPosition(0, @as(f32, @floatFromInt(screen_h)));

{{preview_setup}}{{setup_code}}
    const max_frames = getMaxFrames(allocator);
    const dt: f32 = 1.0 / 60.0;
    var frame: u32 = 0;
    while (frame < max_frames) : (frame += 1) {
{{preview_heartbeat}}{{tick_code}}        g.tick(dt);
{{gui_draw_code}}    }
}
